title: ARI publish.yaml
description: Curation policy for {checkpoint}/ear/. Controls what subset of the EAR is curated into ear_published/ and later sent to a publish backend.

include.description: Glob patterns (relative to ear/) explicitly included in the curated bundle. If empty/absent, nothing is published.

exclude.description: Glob patterns (relative to ear/) excluded from the curated bundle. Built-in deny patterns are applied unconditionally on top of this list.

max_file_mb.description: Hard cap (megabytes, 1024^2 bytes). Any included file larger than this fails curation explicitly (no silent drops).

visibility.description: Initial visibility for the published bundle. Effective publish always starts at 'staged' and is promoted via ari ear promote.

required.description: If true, paper publish is hard-failed when curation/upload fails. If false (default), the Code Availability section is omitted on failure.

auto_promote.description: If true, the pipeline auto-promotes the published artifact (staged -> public) once reproducibility_check passes. Default false to avoid accidental DOI mints / public exposure.

license.description: SPDX-style license identifier (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'CC-BY-4.0'). Surfaced in Zenodo metadata and in the Code Availability section.

backend.description: Default publish backend. CLI --backend overrides this.
